title: Levodopa treatment for low-dose 6-OHDA-treated mice
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9ctvv","text":"This protocol describes a one-week levodopa treatment for mice partially depleted of dopamine.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"703pv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Generate unilaterally low-dose 6-OHDA-lesioned mice (protocol linked below): 6-OHDA mouse model of Parkinson's disease by Beatriz E Nielsen

3 weeks after low-dose 6-OHDA injections, verify unilateral partial dopamine depletion by assessing forelimb use assymetry in the cylinder test (protocol linked below). Motor behavioral assessment by Beatriz E Nielsen
Start daily intraperitoneal (i.p.) injections of 2 mg/kg of L-DOPA (3,4-dihydroxy-L-phenylalanine) plus 12 mg/kg of benserazide hydrochloride dissolved in sterile saline for 7-8 days.
Acute brain slices for electrophysiology recordings (protocol linked below) must be prepared 30min – 1 h after the last L-DOPA administration. Acute Brain Slices by Kelsey Barcomb
creator: Beatriz E Nielsen
